title: phaius incarvillei
intro: Phaius incarvillei, also known as the Incarvillea orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phaius incarvillei include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and soothing digestive issues. Phaius incarvillei can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
